'''Ava Seumanufagai''' (born 3 June 1991) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who is the captain-coach of [[Campbelltown City Kangaroos]]. He has previously played for [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]], [[Wests Tigers]], [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[NRL]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League (Europe) Limited|European Super League]].

==Background==
Seumanufagai was born in [[Wellington]], New Zealand, and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.

He played his junior football for the [[Wainuiomata Lions]] and the [[Hills District Bulls]] before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Seumanufagai played for the Parramatta Eels [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2010 and 2011, scoring 2 tries in 43 games.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/s/seumanufagi_ava/index.php|title=Grandstand Forums|publisher=rleague.com|access-date=2013-09-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100113110246/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/v/vea_atelea/index.php|archive-date=13 January 2010}}</ref> In 2012, Seumanufagai joined the [[Wests Tigers]]. Seumanufagai spent his first season playing [[New South Wales Cup|NSW Cup]], and was a member of the [[Balmain Ryde Eastwood Tigers]] side that lost the NSW Cup grand final to the [[Newtown Jets]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Wayne Cousins |website=Weststigers.com.au |title=Tigers go down fighting to Jets in NSW Cup Grand Final |url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Tigers%20go%20down%20fighting%20to%20Jets%20in%20NSW%20Cup%20Grand%20Final/64669 |access-date=10 November 2012 |date=30 September 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131228214947/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Tigers%20go%20down%20fighting%20to%20Jets%20in%20NSW%20Cup%20Grand%20Final/64669 |archive-date=28 December 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/newtown-jets-defeat-balmain-ryde-eastwood-tigers-in-nsw-cup-to-kick-off-nrl-grand-final-day/story-e6frf3ou-1226484766040|title=Newtown Jets Defeat Balmain Ryde; Eastwood Tigers in NSW Cup to Kick Off NRL Grand Final Day|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref>
[[File:Avaseumanufagai.jpg|thumb|right|Seumanufagai playing for the [[Balmain Tigers]]]]

===2013===
In Round 3, Seumanufagai made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Wests Tigers against his former club, the Parramatta Eels in the Tigers 31–18 win at Leichhardt Oval.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/live-rolling-coverage-of-wests-tigers-v-parramatta-eels/story-e6frexnr-1226603622802|title=Coverage of Tigers v Eels|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> In Round 13 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Seumanufagai scored his first NRL try in the Tigers 20–18 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-rally-for-famous-victory-over-penrith-panthers/story-fnca0von-1226660898600|title=Wests Tigers rally for famous victory, over Penrith Panthers|publisher=The Australian|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> It was his sole try from 17 matches for the [[2013 NRL season|season]].

===2014===
On 30 July, Seumanufagai re-signed with the Tigers on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2014/07/30/seumanufagai_signs_on_at_wests_tigers.html|title=Seumanufagai signs on at Wests Tigers – Tigers|website=Weststigers.com.au|date=30 July 2014|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/ava-seumanufagai-re-signs-tigers/|title=AVA SEUMANUFAGAI RE-SIGNS WITH TIGERS|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=26 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402111157/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/ava-seumanufagai-re-signs-tigers/|archive-date=2 April 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> Coach [[Mick Potter]] said, "Ava has steadily improved as the year has gone on. The players love it when Ava comes on the field, he gives us that lift and really deserves what he is getting."<ref>{{cite news| work= Campbelltown Macarthur Advertiser| title=Seumanufagai and Akauola re-sign with Wests Tigers| url=http://www.macarthuradvertiser.com.au/story/2452185/seumanufagai-and-akauola-re-sign-with-wests-tigers/?cs=1185|access-date=3 March 2015|date=30 July 2014}}</ref> He finished the [[2014 NRL season|year]] having played in 23 matches. He was also named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] train-on squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]], but didn't make the final 24-man squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|title=Samoa train on squad announced|work=Zero Tackle|access-date=12 October 2014|archive-date=14 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2015===
On 31 January and 1 February, Seumanufagai played for the Tigers in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/wests-tigers-2015-nines-squad/|title=WESTS TIGERS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-23|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> He made 23 appearances in the [[2015 NRL season]], all from the interchange bench as well as scoring 1 try for the Tigers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTMtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjAyNjktLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-12-11|access-date=2015-12-27}}</ref> He was named as a reserve in Zero Tackle's Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web| work= The Daily Telegraph|location=London| title=Tonga smash Samoa 36-4| author=Aaron Winder| url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/team-of-the-year-2015-stats-18934/#syemOQ8f6IJgmojw.97|access-date=12 September 2015|date=9 September 2015}}</ref> On 30 September, he re-signed with the Tigers for a further two seasons. Coach [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]] said, "Ava is a tireless worker and a real asset to have on our playing roster. He's only 24 years old, so I'm confident that his best football is still ahead of him and that's really exciting for the club."<ref>{{cite web| website=Weststigers.com.au| title=Wests Tigers re-sign Ava Seumanufagai| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/09/30/wests_tigers_re_sign.html|access-date=30 September 2015|date=30 September 2015}}</ref>

===2016===
In the pre-season, Seumanufagai again played in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/tigers/tigers-place-stars-on-ice-for-nrl-auckland-nines-campaign/news-story/87a0d4ba1b64785792c369f46510e4a0|title=Tigers Place Stars on Ice for NRL Auckland Nines|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2016-02-02}}</ref> He was one of just two Wests Tigers player to appear in every game of the season. Seumanufagai said that he felt his personal game had improved for the season, and was a step in the right direction.<ref>{{cite web| work=weststigers.com.au| title=Unfiltered: Ava Seumanufagai| author=Dan Talintyre| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2016/12/21/unfiltered_ava_seuma.af_unfiltered.html| access-date=9 January 2017| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170109185708/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2016/12/21/unfiltered_ava_seuma.af_unfiltered.html| archive-date=9 January 2017| url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
Seumanufagai again played in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].<ref name="2017 Auckland Nines squads: Every player named for the tournament">{{cite web|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/rugby-league/auckland-nines-squads/|title=2017 Auckland Nines squads: Every player named for the tournament|publisher=The Roar|date=28 February 2017|access-date=16 August 2017}}</ref> On 2 May, he signed a two-year contract with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], starting in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/sharks/cronulla-sharks-announce-signing-of-wests-tigers-prop-ava-seumanufagai/news-story/357351904738708b8a093864f73d4a79|title=NRL: Wests Tigers prop Ava Seumanufagai to join Cronulla Sharks|date=2 May 2017}}</ref> In Round 10 against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Seumanufagai was sinbinned for punching former Tigers teammate [[Robbie Farah]] in the head.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/rabbitohs/south-sydney-dominate-disappointing-wests-tigers-288/news-story/90bc276b5bcd8e4356bdb88ca5c64a81|title=Subscribe – couriermail|website=couriermail.com.au}}</ref> In Round 15 against his future club Cronulla, Seumanufagai played his 100th NRL match in the Tigers 24–22 loss at [[Shark Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/sport/nrl/daniel-mortimer-emerges-from-nrl-wilderness-to-lift-cronulla-sharks-to-win-over-wests-tigers-20170617-gwt4hn.html|title=Daniel Mortimer emerges from NRL wilderness to lift Cronulla Sharks to victory|first=Adam|last=Pengilly|date=17 June 2017|website=Brisbane Times}}</ref> Seumanufagai finished his last season with the Tigers with 2 tries from 18 matches.

===2018===
In Round 1 of the 2018 NRL season, Seumanufagai made his club debut for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], playing off the interchange bench in the 20–14 loss at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=CMWEB_WRE170_a&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.couriermail.com.au%2Fsport%2Fnrl%2Fteams%2Fcowboys%2Fjohnathan-thurston-leads-north-queensland-to-victory-over-cronulla-in-his-300th-nrl-game%2Fnews-story%2F2b3b8b1456bcaccd3ace8bbf96abbb4d&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&nk=b37c957e55ceadf281f41a8a7a9bfc47-1609088588|title=Couriermail.com.au | Subscribe to The Courier Mail for exclusive stories|website=couriermail.com.au|access-date=27 December 2020}}</ref>

Seumanufagai played for Cronulla's feeder club side Newtown in the 2018 [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] grand final against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] at [[Leichhardt Oval]] which Newtown lost 18–12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/canterbury-bulldogs-beat-newtown-jets-to-win-2018-intrust-super-premiership-grand-final/news-story/5194d899167222fcbc817a6dfe96cf09/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs beat Newtown Jets to win 2018 Intrust Super Premiership grand final|website=Fox Sports|date=23 September 2018 }}</ref>

===2019===
Seumanufagai joined [[Leeds Rhinos]] on a two-year deal in May 2019.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Smith |first1=Peter |title=Leeds Rhinos in focus – turning the spotlight on forward Ava Seumanufagai |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-focus-big-name-signing-ava-seumanufagai-super-league-hit-2904372 |access-date=27 December 2020 |work=yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk |date=5 July 2020}}</ref> In his first season he made 13 appearances for the Rhinos scoring one try after making his debut in [[David Furner]]'s last match in charge – a defeat to Salford on 3 May.<ref>{{cite book |editor1-last=Butcher |editor1-first=Tim |editor2-last=Spencer |editor2-first=Daniel |title=Rugby League Yearbook 2019–2020 |year=2019 |isbn=978-190134-7388 |pages=200–201}}</ref>

===2020===
During the disrupted 2020 season Seumanufagai made a further 17 appearances scoring three tries.<ref>{{cite book |editor1-last=Butcher |editor1-first=Tim |editor2-last=Spencer |editor2-first=Daniel |title=Rugby League Yearbook 2020–2021 |year=2020 |isbn=978-190134-7395 |page=189}}</ref> He played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final|Challenge Cup Final]] where Leeds triumphed 17–16 over Salford.<ref>{{cite news |title=Challenge Cup final: Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54555957 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=15 October 2020}}</ref> On 27 December 2020 the club announced that they were releasing Seumanufagai from the final year of his contract due to "family reasons" as the travel restrictions in place were preventing him from seeing his young daughter.<ref>{{cite news |title=Rhinos agree to Seumanufagai release |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55460283 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=27 December 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
He made a total of 16 appearances for Canterbury in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished last and claimed the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/canterbury-bulldogs-set-to-sign-max-king-and-braidon-burns/a9fa54c9-a224-4cc9-ae15-8a5dd1e74275/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs set to sign Rabbitohs star Braidon Burns, Storm prop Max King for 2022 NRL season|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=30 September 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
Seumanufagai played a total of seven matches for Canterbury in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/wapmyddrbadukioadiybr1fs/|title=NRL 2022: Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs season review|website=sportingnews.com|date=12 September 2022 }}</ref>

===2023===
In January, Seumanufagai signed a one-year deal to join the newly promoted [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/23269435.leigh-leopards-sign-prop-ava-seumanufagai-canterbury-bulldogs/|title=Leigh Leopards sign prop Ava Seumanufagai from Canterbury Bulldogs|website=www.leighjournal.co.uk|date=23 January 2023 }}</ref>
He played 15 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>
